zh.Chorley, Lancashire, England, United Kin: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾霍利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn於大約1335年出生在英國藍卡爾布萊恩時，他的父親William Gerard IV24歲，母親Joan de Bryn21歲。他於1354年4月21日之前與Katharine Gerard在英國藍卡爾布萊恩結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1380年11月30日在英國藍卡爾享年46歲。

zh-hans.Chorley, Lancashire, England, United Kin: 英国英格兰藍卡爾霍利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn于大约1335年出生在英国藍卡爾布萊恩时，他的父亲William Gerard IV24岁，母亲Joan de Bryn21岁。他于1354年4月21日之前与Katharine Gerard在英国藍卡爾布萊恩结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1380年11月30日在英国藍卡爾享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Enzo, Gaspare, Luigi, Rocco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Filippo, Francesco, Giovanni, Libero.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the ancient Germanic personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ). This surname is also found in southern France.
German and French (mainly Nord): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name German Gerhard , French Gérard (see Gerard ), using the genitive of the Latinized form Gerardus.

variants.name: Garrison
meanings: English: from a shortened form of Middle English Gerardson, a patronymic from the personal name Gerard + son, or an Anglicized form of its Dutch cognate Gerritsen . The English surname is probably in most cases of Flemish or Dutch origin. It was brought to Britain by immigrants from the Low Countries, especially in the 15th and 16th centuries.
Americanized form of Jewish   Gerson   (see   Gershon  ).
Americanized form of Dutch   Gerritsen  .

variants.name: Garrard
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Gerard  .
Altered form of French Gérard (see Gerard ).
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Geenen
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Geen, a pet form of Gerard .

variants.name: Gerardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Alberto, Alfredo, Aurelio, Carlos, Jesus, Juan, Norberto, Ramon, Tomas, Ana.  Italian Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Enrico, Marco, Marco Antonio.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: English: variant of   Gerard  .

variants.name: Geron
meanings: French (Géron): from the personal name Gero, Gerone, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, as for example Gérard (see Gerard ).
Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
English (Lancashire): of Norman origin, from the personal name Geron of ancient Germanic origin, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gherardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Alessandro, Angelo, Antonio, Donato, Egidio, Ennio, Enzo, Gildo, Lucio, Luigi, Primo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Gherardo, one of themany Italian equivalents of Gerard .

variants.name: Garard
meanings: English (London and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Gerard  .

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn was born about 1335, in Bryn, Lancashire, England, his father, William Gerard IV, was 24 and his mother, Joan de Bryn, was 21. He married Katharine Gerard before 21 April 1354, in Bryn, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 30 November 1380, in Lancashire, England, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn est né environ 1335, à Bryn, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, William Gerard IV, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Joan de Bryn, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Katharine Gerard avant 21 avril 1354, à Bryn, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 30 novembre 1380, à Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn um 1335 in Bryn, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Gerard IV 24 und seine Mutter Joan de Bryn 21. Er hat vor 21. April 1354 in Bryn, Lancashire, England Katharine Gerard geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 30. November 1380 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn è nato il circa 1335 nel Bryn, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, William Gerard IV, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Joan de Bryn, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Katharine Gerard prima del 21 aprile 1354, nel Bryn, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 30 novembre 1380, nel Lancashire, England, all'età di 46 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Peter Gerard of Brynが約1335年にイギリスランカシュアイアブリンで生まれた時，父William Gerard IVは24歳，母Joan de Brynは21歳であった。1354年4月21日以前にイギリスランカシュアイアブリンでKatharine Gerardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1380年11月30日にイギリスランカシュアイアで46歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn(이)가 대략 1335년 영국 랭카실르 브린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Gerard IV(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Bryn(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1354년 4월 21일 이전 영국 랭카실르 브린에서 Katharine Gerard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1380년 11월 30일 영국 랭카실르에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn nasceu aproximadamente 1335, em Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Gerard IV, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Bryn, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Katharine Gerard antes 21 de abril de 1354, em Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 30 de novembro de 1380, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 46 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn родился около 1335 г. в местности брин, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Gerard IV было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Bryn, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Katharine Gerard перед 21 апрель 1354 г. в местности брин, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 30 ноября 1380 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn nació aproximadamente 1335, en Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, William Gerard IV, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Joan de Bryn, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Katharine Gerard antes de 21 de abril de 1354, en Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 30 de noviembre de 1380, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 46 años.
